claimBoundary: The three slots are prepared but have no identified owner, consented project, measured result, feedback, or adoption evidence.

decisionRule.selection: Add a product capability only when the same user problem appears in at least two independent pilot records.

recruitment.message: Improve one existing landing or marketing page through the complete review-to-verified-iteration workflow.
eligibility: The project owner can consent for the named repository and artifact.
One bounded page or user flow can be reviewed and changed.
The project has runnable local verification or a safe preview.
Private data and production credentials can stay outside the evidence bundle.
dataBoundary: Collect only source references, finding decisions, approval events, verification results, and optional quoted feedback approved for retention.
Do not copy secrets, customer records, analytics exports, or private source into this repository.
Keep screenshots and logs in the participant project unless the owner separately approves publication.

measures.method: Derive from canonical UTC pilotStartedAt and firstUsefulArtifactAt timestamps.
Count accepted, rejected, and unresolved workflow findings without an aggregate score.
Count approved, not-required, and pending gates from the exact scope chain.
Use complete, partial, or blocked only when P11 implementation evidence agrees.
Carry unresolved P11 risks forward without rewriting them as passes.
Record feedback only with owner-approved source wording and reference.

stopConditions: Owner authority or consent is missing or ambiguous.
The requested scope expands beyond one bounded page or flow.
Secrets, personal data, customer data, or production credentials enter the evidence path.
A dependency, migration, external write, commit, push, or deployment lacks its separate approval.
Verification cannot distinguish observed behavior from inference.

recruitment.message: Improve one existing web or app workflow whose runtime, responsive, keyboard, and accessibility behavior can be observed.
eligibility: The project owner can consent for the named repository and workflow.
